question.text: A company's IPO has an anchor investor portion of Rs. 200 crore. 40% of this anchor portion is reserved. What percentage of this reserved portion is earmarked for domestic mutual funds?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Of the 40% reserved anchor portion, 33% is earmarked for domestic mutual funds and 7% is reserved for life insurance companies (IRDAI-registered) and pension funds (PFRDA-regulated). If the 7% portion is undersubscribed, it flows to domestic mutual funds. **The Trap:** 50% is tempting because candidates may assume an equal split. The actual split is 33:7 (MFs vs insurance/pension), which totals the 40% reserved portion. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) may reference mutual fund participation in IPOs but not the specific reservation percentages. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Remember "33-7 within 40" — 33% for domestic MFs, 7% for insurance/pension, all within the 40% reserved anchor portion. Undersubscription in the 7% flows to MFs.
